mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp over 3 months

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicate stagflationary pressures that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods with simultaneous GDPC1 decline and CPIAUCSL rise

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k in a month signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of periods with INDPRO growth > 1.5%

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts (HOUST) reflect stronger residential construction activity that boosts demand for goods and services, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 35% of instances where HOUST exceeds 950k

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that pressure wages and inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods with UNRATE below 5%

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rising RSAFS (consumer spending) indicate accommodative policy supporting consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances with FEDFUNDS decline and RSAFS rise

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates shifting demand patterns that create sectoral imbalances, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances with RSAFS increase and HOUST decline
